terms.s1Body: FreeLingo is a free, open-source, self-hosted language learning platform. It is provided as-is, without warranties of any kind. By using this instance you agree to these terms.
terms.s2Intro: You may use FreeLingo solely for personal, non-commercial language learning purposes. You agree not to:
terms.s2i1: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the system.
terms.s2i2: Upload malicious content or attempt to disrupt the service.
terms.s2i3: Share your account credentials with third parties.
terms.s2i4: Use automated scripts or bots to abuse the AI tutoring features.
terms.s3Body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account. The administrator of this instance may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.
terms.s4Body: Conversations with the AI tutor are stored in the database of this instance for learning continuity. The AI may produce inaccurate or unexpected responses — always apply your own judgement. FreeLingo is not responsible for the content generated by the underlying language models.
terms.s5Body: FreeLingo is designed to run on infrastructure you or the instance operator controls. All data remains on the server hosting this instance. The project authors have no access to any data on third-party deployments.
terms.s6Body: This service is provided without any uptime guarantees. The operator may take the instance offline for maintenance, updates, or any other reason at any time.
terms.s7Body: FreeLingo and its contributors are not liable for any direct, indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from your use of, or inability to use, this service.
terms.s8Body: These terms may be updated at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

privacy.s1Body: FreeLingo is a self-hosted application. All data described in this policy is stored exclusively on the server infrastructure operated by the person or organization hosting this instance. The FreeLingo project authors have no access to any personal data on deployments they do not operate.
privacy.s2Intro: When you create an account and use FreeLingo, the following data is stored in the instance's PostgreSQL database:
privacy.s2i1: Username, display name, and email address.
privacy.s2i2: Hashed password (bcrypt — your plain-text password is never stored).
privacy.s2i3: Native language preference and learning settings.
privacy.s2i5: Chat and conversation history with the AI tutor.
privacy.s2i6: Flashcard decks, study progress, and spaced-repetition data.
privacy.s3Intro: Your data is used exclusively to provide the FreeLingo learning experience:
privacy.s3i1: Authenticating you and maintaining your session.
privacy.s3i2: Personalizing your study plan and tutor interactions.
privacy.s3i3: Tracking your learning progress over time.
privacy.s3Footer: Your data is never sold, rented, or shared with third-party marketing services.
privacy.s4Body: If this instance is configured to use a remote AI provider (such as OpenAI or Anthropic), your tutor conversation messages may be sent to that provider's API to generate responses. This is subject to that provider's own privacy policy. By default, FreeLingo is designed to run with a local Ollama instance and no data leaves the server.
privacy.s5Body: FreeLingo uses short-lived JWT access tokens (stored in memory) and a long-lived refresh token stored as an httpOnly cookie. The refresh token is stored in Redis with a 30-day expiry and is rotated on every use. Logging out invalidates the token immediately.
privacy.s6Body: Your data is retained for as long as your account exists. You can permanently delete your account and all associated data at any time from the Settings page. Account deletion is immediate and irreversible.
privacy.s7Body: For any privacy-related concerns about this specific instance, please contact the administrator of the server you are using. For questions about the FreeLingo project itself, visit the project repository on GitHub.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a_start: Start with the Assessment. It detects your English level (A1–C2) and your strengths and weaknesses. With that result you can generate a personalized Study Plan that fills your Dashboard with daily lessons. Without the Assessment, the Dashboard is empty.
faq.workflowStep1: Register and select your native language
faq.workflowStep2: Take the Assessment → your CEFR level is detected
faq.workflowStep3: Generate your Study Plan from the Dashboard
faq.workflowStep4: Complete your daily lessons (START button in the Dashboard)
faq.workflowStep5: Use Flashcards to train vocabulary in parallel
faq.workflowStep6: Practice free conversation with the AI Tutor
faq.a_language: English only, for now. The native language you choose at registration is used exclusively for flashcard translations and tutor feedback — it does not change the target language.
faq.a_assessment: A 20-question AI-generated test covering grammar, vocabulary, reading comprehension, and sentence transformation, with difficulty ranging from A1 to C1. When you finish, you get your CEFR level, a score, and an analysis of your strengths and weaknesses. This result feeds into the Study Plan and generated Flashcards.
faq.q_studyPlan: What is the Study Plan and how do I generate one?
faq.a_studyPlan: After the Assessment, go to the Dashboard and click Generate Study Plan. A week-by-week plan with daily lessons tailored to your CEFR level and goals will be created automatically. Each lesson includes multiple-choice and free-writing exercises evaluated by AI.
faq.a_flashcards: A vocabulary training system using the SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm. Generate cards on any topic (e.g. "business", "phrasal verbs", "food") with a definition, example sentence, and translation into your native language. The system calculates when to show each card again just before you forget it. Completely independent from the Study Plan.
faq.q_tutor: How does the AI Tutor work?
faq.a_tutor: A free-form streaming chat. The tutor knows your CEFR level and weak points to tailor its responses. Ask grammar questions, request explanations, practice writing in English, or just have a conversation. Conversations are saved and accessible from the sidebar across sessions.
faq.q_providers: Which AI providers does FreeLingo support?
faq.provider_ollama: Local, free. Requires Ollama running on the host. Recommended model: gemma3:12b. May be slow without a GPU.
faq.provider_openai: OpenAI API (GPT-4o, etc.). Fast and reliable. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY.
faq.a_invite: Yes. From Admin → Users the admin can create users directly or generate single-use invite links that expire in 48 hours. Public registration is disabled by default (ALLOW_REGISTRATION=false).
faq.q_password: How do I change my password or native language?
faq.a_password: Go to <settingsLink>Settings</settingsLink>. You can update your display name, native language, and password from there.
